items.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
items.summary: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

Samsung estimates Q2 operating profit of ~$58.44B, a 19-fold jump from a year earlier and above a ~$57.02B estimate, and revenue up 129% YoY to ~$111.7B  —  Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) on Tuesday forecast a 19-fold jump in second-quarter operating profit from a year earlier, as AI-driven demand continued to lift memory chip prices.

items.title: Anthropic researchers detail J-space, a small set of neural patterns in Claude that reveals internal thoughts that don't appear in the model's output (Anthropic)
items.summary:
Anthropic:
Anthropic researchers detail J-space, a small set of neural patterns in Claude that reveals internal thoughts that don't appear in the model's output  —  As you read this sentence, circuits in your brain are adjusting your posture, controlling your breathing, and transforming lines and curves on the screen into recognizable words.

Instead of running all over town, try switching things up and getting all your shopping done under one roof at Costco. Right now, you can get a 1-Year Costco Gold Star membership plus a $50 Digital Costco Shop Card*, and it’s still only $65.

items.title: WD’s compact 2TB portable SSD hits its lowest price since the hike at $200 (Reg. $310)
items.summary: Woot is now offering the 2TB Western Digital Elements SE portable SSD for $199.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). That’s down from the $310 price this model has been fetching at Amazon lately, knocking just over $110 off for roughly 35% in savings. While it regularly sold closer to $125 until November of last year, storage pricing has steadily climbed since then, with this particular model eventually reaching the $300 range. Today’s Woot deal marks the lowest price we have tracked since that hike, making it a notable chance to score a compact 2TB portable SSD for far less than the usual rates these drives have been fetching lately. Head below for more details.
